sentences.text: Good morning!
Good morning, how are you?
I'm doing alright, how are you?
Doing well, doing well.
It's almost the weekend, so….
I know.
And a long one at that, so….
It is.
Do you have any big plans this weekend?
So I live in Columbus, Ohio, so having some folks over for the… the Buckeye game, and….
Yeah.
Other than that, just kind of laying low.
How about you?
Same, I'm in Tennessee, so… the Tennessee game's on.
We're not… we don't have as big of a game as you guys, but, … I don't know, I think, I think you guys will end up destroying Texas.
That's… that's the goal.
You didn't really lose too many people last year, or into the draft this year… past year, right?
Right, yeah.
So….
Wide receiver university over there.
There you go.
So, have you always lived in Ohio?
I have, yeah.
Okay.
I was born a couple hours north of Columbus, and before I ever even started school, moved to a suburb just outside of Columbus, so… been here ever since.
Nice, nice.
That's, I almost moved to Columbus.
So, I'm from Tennessee, and then I got a job in Indianapolis.
Okay.
And when I got promoted there, they were like, well, we're gonna relocate you to Columbus, Ohio.

I'm like, okay, cool, that sounds like a cool area to go and see and live in for a little bit and whatnot, and then… Two weeks later, they came back, they were like, never mind, Columbus is off the table, but we're gonna go to Lexington, Kentucky.
And I was like.
Oh, okay.
and the SEC, I guess, but….
Yep.
So… Well, nice.
Well, thank you for hopping on with me today.
I know you're busy and have a lot on your plate, so… Yeah.

… I guess we can kind of dive in, because I know you got a busy day, and you probably want to head out a little early for the weekend, but, I guess the way I envision the call going is I had some questions for you about your portfolio.
I'm sure you want to know exactly what we offer, so explain that to you, and then we can see where to go from there.
That's perfect.
Sweet.
I tried doing as much research as possible, just so I didn't have to ask you a million and one questions, but, do you guys have, like, 25 properties right now?
24, okay.
And then, all of them are located in Ohio, pretty much around Columbus, correct?
Yeah, yeah, we can… I can get to any of our properties in, like, 25 minutes or less.
Oh, nice, okay.
That's really fortunate for you, at least you're not having to drive to Kentucky, or… Exactly.
Yeah, we've always… our owners are, … from Westerville, which is where our corporate office is.

Born and raised here, and so we're just 100% locally owned and operated, and everything's here, and it's kind of been… for us, what's worked for us, you know, to have local management that can show up at a moment's notice, you know, to address whatever the case might be.
So, yeah, our whole portfolio is just right… right around here in Columbus.
Yeah, that's very fortunate, and … Champion owns all of them, correct?
Okay, so no third party, so that's even better for you.
You don't even have to go to multiple ownership groups.
Correct.
We didn't start that way.

We started, with some third-party management, but as we got going, we just liked the control of if we wanted to replace roofs or do paving or, you know, whatever, that, we could just say yes and didn't have to ask anybody for permission or for some money, so… So yeah, we're 100%, owner.
We own everything we manage.
That's good.
And you were there whenever you guys were still third-partying some things?
Yeah, so I've been with Champion for 14 years, and we're 15 years old as a company, so, been here pretty much since the beginning.
Gotcha.
I'm sure that was a frustrating thing, though, like, you need to replace the roof, you're like, yeah, well, let me think about it.
What's there to think about?
Like, this has to get done.
Yeah.
Cool, okay, when it comes to the advertising, I know you're using a little bit of Apartments.
com, what else do you do?
We partner… so… instead of having kind of, like, a blanket approach for our entire portfolio, I've really tried to take a look at what each of our communities need.
So, our larger properties are our new developments we have with Apartments.
com.
We advertise all of our properties on ApartmentList, just because it's pay-per-lease, and that's… to me, I like that.
That makes sense to me.
We use Zillow, on a handful of our properties.
We do our own Google Ads.
For our whole portfolio as well.
And then we're just gonna start doing a little bit of advertising with Yelp.
Which we haven't done before, that'll be new.
Part of the reason why I was interested in reaching out is two of our communities, it's called the District at Berkshire, and then River Park of Dublin.
They're two brand new developments.

We built them, they're both open now, so we're in the midst of the lease-up, and as seasonality shifts here, as we're headed into the holidays, just trying to look at New ways that we can, add some exposure specifically to those two properties.

… and potentially some others as well, but really those two are my main focus that we're trying… we've got some just occupancy goals that we're trying to hit by the end of the year, or early first quarter of next year.
And so, just wanted to, you know, try some new things.
So we're doing some for those two properties specifically, some advertising with iHeartRadio, some podcast radios.

We've done some streaming, you know, CTV, partnerships with the local, television station, with NBC4, so… And that's all not necessarily something that we typically do for our whole massive portfolio, but we've been doing with these new developments in an effort to try to have some more exposure specifically for them.
Yeah, no, that makes sense.
You gotta kind of adapt to what you got going on.
Right.
I've never heard of anybody doing iHeartRadio before.
Oh, no?
No, I… I've heard… so I've been doing… I've been in this industry now for, like, three and a half years.
Okay.
But I've only heard one other person say something along those lines, and they said Spotify.
Oh, okay.
And I'm like, that's interesting, because I… that's just, like, not something I would think of.
Yeah, so our iHeartRadio, we do both broadcasts, so, like, actually on the local radio stations here, but then they also take our spots and put them on iHeartRadio and other streaming services.

So if you're listening to a podcast on Spotify, and you're in the zip code zone of where these properties are located, and you meet kind of, like, a demographic profile that we've set up, then you have the chance to hear our ad.
So we're doing that as well.
But the local radio stations is really common here.
It's something that, a lot of our local competitors do as well.
Okay.
See, I've heard local radio, and, like, newspapers, but, like, when you said iHeartRadio, I'm, like, thinking, okay, podcasts, like, podcast commercials, and I… that one I haven't heard.
So that's very unique.
Cool.
Well, thank you for sharing all of that with me.
what I'll do is I'll kind of dive in, show you where we kind of differ, and how we can help you stand out some, and… yeah.
I'll show and tell as well.
I don't like just talk… just talking, I gotta show pictures.
I'm almost like a little kid with a book.
I'd rather look at… It has a nice picture in it, then… Than just words.
So, can you see this okay?
I can.
Okay, so, rentable in itself, we're an ILS, just like your Apartments.
com or your Zillows.
We do differ in a couple of ways.
One is the large… wide-cast net of a network that we're able to reach.
And then the other one is, how cost-effective we are.
We're pennies to the dollar compared to what Apartments.
com and Zillow cost.
When it comes down to everything we offer, we… we fully integrate with Yardi.
It looked like that was your software that you're using.
So, anytime you're updating information on your end, it'll pull and it'll reflect on ours.
Pricing, availability, pictures, you name it.
So once we have those properties, we're gonna list them on our site itself.
From there, we'll syndicate it out to our massive syndicate network, and when I say our massive syndicate network, we go literally everywhere except for Apartments.
com.
So, what we do, though, is we have an AI tool working in the background to make sure we're not duplicating posts.
So, like, since you're a portfolio-wide with apartment list, we wouldn't push there and duplicate any listings.
Properties on Zillow, we wouldn't push, duplicate there.
But we'd fill in the gaps for, like, let's say… In your case, 12 of your 24 properties were using apartment list, for an example.
We wouldn't push there for those 12, but for the remaining 12, we would still get you there.
Okay.
So yeah, so we're really… our focus here is filling in the gaps, getting your properties exposure on all the different sites that they're not already on.
And then on top of that, we have an all-inclusive targeted social ad.
So we're getting your properties on Facebook and Instagram, reaching out to renters versus waiting for them to come to you.
And the way they're working is very similar to, like, your Google Ad approach and your iHeartRadio.

ad approach, so anytime somebody is… these are… these are, like, redirecting, I guess, so anytime somebody goes on, like, Google and says, show me apartments in Westerville, next time they get on their Facebook or Instagram, within 45 seconds of scrolling, we're gonna hit them with a rentable ad that looks like this.
It'll have pictures of your individual property, details about it, lowest advertised price, if it's pet friendly, if there's a pool, whatever it may be.
And then, where we differ from a lot of other social ads.
A lot of other companies will do usually one of two things.
They'll either redirect them to their personal site, and then that renter can see all the available ads that they're listing for other companies as well.
Or, they'll redirect them to a property website, and at that point, you kind of lose track.
Did that person become a lead?
Did it come from here?
We don't really know.
So, all of ours are lead generating.
So, once somebody clicks learn more, we're going to keep them here.
They're gonna fill out a guest card form with their name, their email, and their phone number, and we'll deliver that directly into your Yardi system.
Or to an email address, whichever you prefer.
And that's how all of our leads would come in.
Rentable leads, our syndicate network, our social ads, they'll all come in, we can deliver them directly into your Yardi CRM, or to, an on-site leasing email.
Okay.
Can you do more than one?

So, like, a lot of our… we have typically two lead destinations, one that's a tracking email that would dump into Yardi, but kind of as a fail-safe, a secondary one that just goes to, like, the property email address.
Yep, we can do both.
Okay.
We can definitely do both.
… And then the other thing with our system.
So, we like using AI.

Ai is the way of the world nowadays, unfortunately, but that same tool that's making sure we're not duplicating your listings is also making sure that we're getting your properties where they're getting the most visibility, and it's producing you the actual results.
So… In this case, we'll say, okay, let's say we get you on, like, 30 different sites.

It'll say for your proper… one property is, you know, here's your top 3 sites, and then it'll say, here's your bottom three, so that way, instead of us trying to get results out of these bottom 3 that just aren't doing anything, we can put that spend towards the top 3 sites to double down efforts there.
But it can also differ per property, because the property in Westerville may not be the same as, like, downtown Columbus.

So, and it'll kind of adjust things that way, and we can also gear it more towards quality versus just quantity, so it'll say, you know, this site may be receiving more leads, but this site, out of the leads that you're getting, is getting more leases or conversions to tours, whatever it may be.
Okay.
So, I'll pause for a second, because I feel like I've said a lot.
How does this compare to what you're currently doing?
So I didn't mention it when you asked me in terms of, like, an advertising strategy, but we also manage some of our own Facebook and Instagram ads.
Again, we're just doing it for those two new development properties.
Yeah.
it's not… it's not necessarily something that we're doing for the whole portfolio, but we do set those up kind of ourselves.
I think we've started doing some retargeting ads through our Google Ads campaign, but again, that kind of lives just within… within Google still.
So, I think it's great.
I think it's a… we've noticed an uptick in traffic.
We've had leases that have said they've found us on Instagram, or they found us on Facebook, so I think it's… it's a nice strategy.
So that's just included.
That's not an additional spend?
Okay.
It's all-inclusive.
We have, like, a one-size-fits-all package, basically.
Okay.
Okay.
So… so I guess, do you have any other questions for me?
Not yet, -
Okay.
The, … I guess in terms of success stories, usually I talk about Cornerstone Residential, but they're not really in your area.
We're working with, in your area, companies like PK Management, Woda Cooper.
Coastal Ridge.
some of those.
Water Cooper's been on with us now for quite some time, but they've seen a lot of success in your area.
Crystal Ridge is a… is a good comparison from a management company standpoint.
We've actually purchased apartment communities from them, and vice versa.
We've sold some of our properties to them.
So they're a little bit newer in the Columbus market, but very similar in terms of Of portfolio and product.
Gotcha.
Okay, that's… that's good to know.
We have… like, a handful of properties on with them.
Woda Cooper, though, I think we have, like, closer to, like.
15 to 20 properties.
I actually haven't heard of them.
Really, they are… Where is it?
They're out of Columbus.
Okay, I wonder if it's just a different, like, product class.
It might be.
I didn't look that part up.
Most of… most of our communities are all, … considered, like, a… either A+, A-plus to B+, product.
And we are not downtown at all, and we don't… and we don't do any student housing, and so we're really… we're kind of in the suburbs of Columbus.
And so, if they're down… they might be some, you know, more downtown, or in that student housing, or a different product class, but I have not heard of them before.
Gotcha.
Yeah, they look like they're more of, like, the B class.
But they have properties…
Minnesota, Iowa, Illinois, all the way over to PA and Virginia.
So they have properties, like, on a good chunk of the country, which may be why they may not have, like, solely in Columbus the same way, … The same way Coastal Ridge.
Yeah.
Set up.
… I'm trying to think of some other ones that might be similar portfolios, like MA… so we're working with MAA out of Nashville.
They're not really, like, in the Columbus area, but….
Yeah.
That's a similar portfolio, … Mostly A Class B.

Before we had… before I had responded to you about setting up the call, I did… I hopped on the site and just kind of searched some of the cities that were… that where our properties are located, and… what I liked, actually, is there were some properties I recognized, like the property names, but several of our main competitors, I don't think, are on there, which I think is a nice opportunity for us.
Phew.
So… Yeah, okay.
Cool.
I guess the only other thing is pricing, then.
When it comes to pricing, we're only… $1.
99 a month per property.
Okay.
We typically require a 5-property minimum when coming on board with us, but that gets you everything from our rentable network, all of our partnerships, the included paid social.
Ai tool, get you our backend dashboard.
We get you everything.
Okay.
Do you…
have… so, like, we have a third property that is currently under development, but it's not going to be… we physically can't even be on-site, probably, until after the first of the year.
Do you offer, like, any type of a, like, a waitlist function for a property that's under construction?
Like, a different style of listing.
Yeah, so we don't have… like, a waitlist, in a sense.
Like, we can still do the advertising for that property and send it over to you.
Like, we can put… So, like, we can… we can edit, like, the posting a little bit, so, like, we could put, like.
building waitlist, or something next to it, like, in development, whatever you need to make sure, like, a renter knows, like, hey, it's not ready just yet.
And then we advertise, like.
If they're up-and-coming availability, like, when that availability is.
Okay.
Do you require an integration with Yardi, or can a listing be manually built?

It can be manually built, and then, like, in your case, because I'm assuming it's probably not in Yardi yet, because it's not finished, we can manually build that one, and then whenever it's ready and in Yardi, all we gotta do is flip on the feed, and then things will update itself manual, or automatically.
Okay.
We definitely… so, a handful of our properties We probably have.
at least 4 that are over 350 units, and so… and those ones, it's just kind of… we always need traffic, you know, it just never, never fails, and so… The 5 property minimum doesn't concern me.
I definitely think, while, you know, the 2 is what I'm most interested in, I think I could fill those other 3 spots with some of those larger properties.
Okay.
Alright, so $199 a month.
In terms of a contract, is it a… A certain term, is it month-to-month?
We normally do annuals.
Right now we're doing as short as 6 months.
Still for $1.
Yeah.
Okay.
So would it be 6-month or 12-month?
Or, like, you do 6, 7, 8, 9?
We do 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, all the way up to 24, honestly.
Whatever you prefer.
There is no implementation cost, there's no startup fees, for… depending on how many properties, implementation typically takes between 5 to 10 business days.
Our team's usually really quick with it, so I'd say, like, on the latter side of that.

And then when I send over an agreement, basically, if… once it's signed, implementation will reach out to you within 24 to 48 hours, and then from there, what they'll do is they'll verify that all the property information I put in was correct, like, in terms of that I grabbed the correct property from online, and then they'll confirm where you want the leads being sent.
And then… Yeah, and then they walk you through how to turn on the Yardi integration.
Okay.
… All right.
Well, I think what I'd like to do is kind of take a look from a… from a budgetary standpoint, I lean more towards 6 months.
What would that be?
September, October, November, December, January, February?
Just as a… give it a… give it a try and see how things go.
Yeah.
and I'll… let me try to identify what these other three properties would be in addition to those two new developments.
I would… any kind of new partner, our legal team always wants to take a look at the contract, so that would be… but they're… they're really quick.
Typically, it's a day or two turnaround to get me any of their feedback.
So that would be, you know, a momentary, I guess, pause on my part, is just to get their feedback there.
I'd have to initiate new tracking phone numbers and everything with Yardi, just so that, you know, I'm ready there.
But other than that, I mean, I think it's a no-brainer, especially for these two new development properties.
The cost, you write is incredibly minimal, and… which is great.
So, that's what I'm thinking.
I think 5 properties, 6 months, give it a try, and go from there.
Perfect, that works.
And I can send you over a sample agreement as well for your legal team to look over, if that makes it quicker on your end, too.
Yeah, they'd probably want to see the actual… the actual thing with the actual terms.
It'll be, sorry, it'll be the exact thing with the exact terms, but instead of me putting, like, the name of the 5 properties in there, it'll just say, like, 5 properties with yada yada.
Okay.
That's fine then, yeah.
When we're doing a larger agreement, let me tell you, let me make sure I get this right.
We operate as the Champion Companies, but, like, our legal name is Champion Real Estate Services.
Okay.
… I think it's Champion Real Estate Services, Inc.
Let me find… let me find an agreement and just confirm, because that's the one sticking point they always have.
At least your company's legal name is very similar to, like, the name it's called.
I saw an agreement the other day that I had to put together, and it was, like… I don't even remember what company it was for at this point, but their, like, legal name was, like, 15 words in there.
Oh, gosh.
I'm like, what in the heck is this?
But….
Yes, Champion Real Estate Services, Inc.
Okay, I got that.
Perfect.
… In terms of the 6-month contract, do you guys… does it require, like, a 60-day written notice to cancel?
Does it automatically roll month to month after the 6th month?
Does it… would you reach out as far as a renewal?
How does that work?
Yeah, so typically they renew for an annual.
I can change it to a month-to-month if that's preferred for you.
Okay.
It requires a 30-day notice.
Okay.
Prior to canceling, and then, do you need a cl… because… how often do you guys sell properties?
it often.
I, I mean… No.
I mean, it… kind of.
We're super transactional as a business, but the market hasn't been there to really sell.
We are in contract to sell 4 of our communities currently.
But there's not… there hasn't been talks of….
much else.
I'm assuming, though… I guess, how does that work from your guys' side?

So that's what I was gonna say, is I can put a clause in the agreement that it's, like, a 30-day out prop sold, where you just have to let us know 30 days in advance and provide proof of it to us, and then we kind of void that agreement for it.
Okay, that would be good as a just-in-case.

Again, I think the properties that I will add, we would be ones that we hold onto, but just in case… And I think, yes, if there's the option for it to just go month-to-month at the end of the 6 months, that would be… Preferred at least to just have it in there, and then to touch base, you know, at that time, and maybe sign something longer term, or add more properties, or whatever that might look like.
And we can definitely do that, and you and I will be in constant communication from here on out, too, so….
At the end of that 6 months.
if I'm… if I'm like, hey, these 4 properties are going really well, but the 5th property, I just don't think it's necessary, can I go below the 5 property minimum, or does The remainder of life.
Yeah, you can.
You can go below.

Just, like, starting, they… they put the rule in because what happened in the past was, like, companies would give us, like, one property was their worst property, nothing ever worked on it, and then they're like, oh, well, you guys don't work, and they would never try us on anything else.
So, it just kind of gives a company now, like, a good… A better perspective.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Meh, that make sense?
Okay.
Well, wonderful.
Cool.
Well, I will get to work on, like, the sample proposal that's….
Okay.
gonna be exactly what the proposal looks like, in a sense, other than the property names.
And then I'll send you a recap email as well, just following up the call.
And… yeah, I look forward to working with you.
In terms of, properties, I guess, when would you expect to have those over to me?
Probably early next… well, let's say middle next week, just since we're closed on Monday.
Yeah, yeah.
Okay.

But I'll… I'll pull some numbers today, both from kind of a leasing and occupancy standpoint, as well as just take a look at some of the property budgets and see who's got room for it between now and the end of the year, beginning of next year, and go from there.
Cool, that works.
Okay?
That works.
Awesome.
Well, thank you for your time, Brittany, and I'll get that information over here to you shortly.
Yeah, sounds good.
Thank you so much.
You're welcome.
Have a good one.
